title: Podman tutorial
body:
How to build, install, and use podman on Fedora.  The ubuntu instructions
are currently commented out due to issue #166.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: In Buildah, we've a tutorials directory under the docs directory.  I'd suggest moving this new tutorial to a similar structure for podman.

I will take a look at this a little late today. I noticed a couple of
things that probably need to be more explicit.

William

On Fri, Dec 29, 2017 at 11:53 AM Tom Sweeney <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> *@TomSweeneyRedHat* commented on this pull request.
> ------------------------------
>
> In podman_tutorial.md
> <https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/170#discussion_r159092514>:
>
> > +```
> +Note the Container ID returned by that command.
> +
> +### Listing running containers
> +Once you have created or run a container, you can list the known containers with the *ps* command.
> +```
> +# sudo podman ps
> +```
> +
> +Note: If you add *-a* to the *ps* command, it podman will show all containers.
> +
> +### Executing a command in a running container
> +You can use the *exec* subcommand to execute a command in a running container.  In this case, we
> +need to install iproute in the container so we can obtain its IP address.
> +```
> +# sudo podman exec -t <container_id> dnf install iproute
>
> What you see in my prior reply is what I got, error synchronizing the
> updates repo.
